subject: Databases
title: Data Integrity Matters
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Data Integrity Matters.

hook.text: A user's age was -5. Another had an email of 12345. No constraints meant garbage data slipped in.

buildup.text: Data integrity means every value in the database is accurate, consistent, and valid.

discovery.text: Databases enforce integrity through types, NOT NULL, UNIQUE, CHECK constraints, and foreign keys.

twist.text: App-level validation can be bypassed. Database constraints are the last line of defense.

climax.text: Both layers matter: validate in the app for user feedback, enforce in the database for safety.

punchline.text: Trust but verify. The database is the final gatekeeper.

quiz.question: Why should you enforce constraints at the database level, not just the app?
options: App validation can be bypassed; database constraints are the last line of defense
Database constraints make the app faster
